coverage_model.description: NHS provides universal coverage funded through general taxation. NICE technology appraisals determine which medicines the NHS must fund.
coverage_model.key_gap: Access delays between NICE recommendation and local formulary listing

countries.notes: Devolved health systems (Scotland/SMC, Wales/AWMSG) may have separate HTA processes

coverage_model.description: Statutory health insurance (GKV) covers ~90% of population. AMNOG process determines added benefit and price negotiation.
coverage_model.key_gap: Opt-out possible after AMNOG if price negotiation fails (rare but significant)

countries.notes: Immediate market access at launch price for first 12 months (free pricing period), then AMNOG assessment determines reimbursement price

coverage_model.description: Assurance Maladie provides universal coverage. HAS Transparency Committee assesses clinical benefit (SMR) and improvement (ASMR) to determine reimbursement and pricing.
coverage_model.key_gap: Long delay between MA and actual reimbursement listing (average 400-500 days)

countries.notes: Dual rating system: SMR (absolute clinical benefit, determines reimbursement rate 15-100%) and ASMR (improvement vs existing therapy, determines price premium)

steps.label: EMA/MHRA Marketing Authorization
steps.likely_blocker: Clinical data package incomplete or negative benefit-risk
steps.notes: UK uses both national (MHRA) and EMA routes depending on product

steps.label: NICE Technology Appraisal Scoping
steps.notes: NICE can initiate before MA is granted via early engagement

steps.label: NICE Evidence Submission
steps.likely_blocker: Health economic model not accepted; ICER above willingness-to-pay threshold
steps.notes: Manufacturer submits clinical evidence + economic model. Evidence Review Group provides independent critique.

steps.label: NICE Appraisal Committee Meeting
steps.likely_blocker: Unfavourable ICER; uncertainty in clinical evidence; lack of comparator data
steps.notes: Committee issues Appraisal Consultation Document (ACD) or Final Appraisal Document (FAD)

steps.label: NICE Final Guidance Publication
steps.likely_blocker: Appeal by manufacturer or consultees
steps.notes: If recommended, NHS must fund within 90 days of publication

steps.label: NHS England Commercial Agreement
steps.likely_blocker: Budget impact concerns; managed access agreement negotiations
steps.notes: Patient access schemes, managed access agreements, and commercial arrangements negotiated here

steps.label: Local Formulary Listing
steps.likely_blocker: Local budget constraints; pathway redesign needed
steps.notes: Even with NICE recommendation, local uptake can vary significantly

steps.label: Patient Access
steps.likely_blocker: Prescriber awareness; patient identification
steps.notes: End of pathway — medicine available to eligible patients

steps.label: EMA Marketing Authorization
steps.likely_blocker: Clinical data package incomplete
steps.notes: Most new active substances use EMA centralized procedure

steps.label: Market Launch at Free Price
steps.notes: Germany guarantees immediate market access at manufacturer-set price for first 12 months (unique in EU)

steps.label: AMNOG Dossier Submission
steps.likely_blocker: Incomplete dossier; wrong comparator chosen
steps.notes: Manufacturer must submit benefit dossier at launch. G-BA assigns to IQWiG or does own assessment.

steps.label: IQWiG Benefit Assessment
steps.likely_blocker: No additional benefit vs appropriate comparator
steps.notes: IQWiG assesses added benefit on 6-point scale: major, considerable, minor, non-quantifiable, no added benefit, less benefit

steps.label: G-BA Added Benefit Decision
steps.likely_blocker: G-BA disagrees with IQWiG; assigns no added benefit
steps.notes: G-BA makes final determination of added benefit at 6 months post-launch. Determines whether price negotiation or reference pricing applies.

steps.label: GKV-SV Price Negotiation
steps.likely_blocker: Negotiation impasse → arbitration board
steps.notes: If added benefit confirmed: price negotiation. If no added benefit: assigned to reference price group. Manufacturer may opt out (withdraw).

steps.label: Negotiated Price Takes Effect
steps.likely_blocker: Manufacturer opt-out (product withdrawal from German market)
steps.notes: Negotiated price replaces free-pricing period. Retroactive rebates may apply from month 7.

steps.label: Patient Access at Reimbursed Price
steps.likely_blocker: Prescribing quotas (Richtgrößen); aut idem substitution for generics
steps.notes: Patient has been accessing since step 2; this step reflects access at final negotiated price

steps.label: EMA Marketing Authorization
steps.likely_blocker: Clinical data package incomplete
steps.notes: Most innovative medicines use EMA centralized procedure

steps.label: Early Access (Autorisation d'Accès Précoce)
steps.likely_blocker: Product does not meet presumed innovation threshold
steps.notes: Optional. Available for innovative medicines with unmet need before formal HTA. Replaced former ATU system in 2021.

steps.label: HAS Transparency Committee Assessment
steps.likely_blocker: Insufficient SMR rating (insufficient clinical benefit)
steps.notes: Dual rating: SMR (clinical benefit, 4 levels) determines if reimbursed and at what rate. ASMR (improvement, 5 levels I-V) determines price premium.

steps.label: SMR/ASMR Rating Decision
steps.likely_blocker: SMR insuffisant = no reimbursement; ASMR V = no improvement = reference pricing
steps.notes: SMR sufficient → reimbursed. ASMR I-III → significant improvement → higher price possible. ASMR IV-V → price must be below or equal to comparators.

steps.label: CEPS Price Negotiation
steps.institution: CEPS (Comité Économique des Produits de Santé)
steps.likely_blocker: Price agreement cannot be reached; budget impact too high
steps.notes: CEPS negotiates manufacturer price based on ASMR, comparator prices, EU reference prices, and expected volumes. Can take 6-18 months.

steps.label: Journal Officiel Publication
steps.notes: Official publication of reimbursement listing and price in the Journal Officiel

steps.label: UNCAM Reimbursement Rate Setting
steps.notes: Reimbursement rate set based on SMR: 65% (important), 30% (moderate), 15% (low). 100% for irreplaceable and costly medicines (ALD).

steps.label: Patient Access
steps.likely_blocker: Prescriber awareness; hospital listing committees for hospital medicines
steps.notes: Medicine available on prescription. Hospital medicines may require additional T2A listing.
